libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Tests that call ByteBuddyAgent.install(...) with a fake AttachmentProvider or fake VirtualMachine must make the fake loadAgent/loadAgentPath path initialize net.bytebuddy.agent.Installer with a non-null java.lang.instrument.Instrumentation, for example via Installer.agentmain/premain using a minimal RecordingInstrumentation. Byte Buddy 1.11.17 verifies getInstrumentation after attachment, so merely recording that loadAgent was called is not enough. If a test intentionally exercises real self-attachment instead of fakes, run it on a JDK with the jdk.attach module; set jdk.attach.allowAttachSelf=true only when the test specifically requires direct self-attach rather than Byte Buddy's external-attach fallback.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #6309 [Automation] java run fails for net.bytebuddy:byte-buddy-agent:1.11.17; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Real dynamic agent loading is JVM- and version-sensitive and may emit warnings or fail under stricter future JDK defaults.

Native Image execution is unlikely to support real JVM attach behavior, so coverage should prefer deterministic fake attachment paths unless local Gradle and Native Image verification prove otherwise.
The artifact has provided/optional JNA-related paths for emulated attachment; do not test those paths unless intentionally adding JNA, which is not required for normal coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed, but meaningful byte-buddy-agent tests must handle JVM attach semantics and Byte Buddy 1.11.17's post-attach Instrumentation check.
